pages: On a Sunday, a handmade sign stood in front of the community center. 'The Classroom for a Brighter Future.' The event was hosted by Garu-chan, a single mother kangaroo. The theme was family building and childcare support. Although the words seemed radical, the purpose was realistic.

'The era where others decide whether to have children or not is over. Let's create a society where we can choose.' Garu-chan declared at the beginning. The participants were diverse, ranging from young couples to singles and grandparents. The atmosphere was slightly tense.

She presented statistics on childcare slots, rent, work styles, and community support systems. Rather than idealistic theories, she discussed practical life planning. 'Family building won't last with just enthusiasm. We need systems and companions.' The audience's nods of agreement increased.

During the Q&A session, some harsh comments were made. 'It's impossible to raise a child alone.' Garu-chan didn't deny it. 'That's why we need to create a system where we don't have to bear it alone. I was helped many times along the way.' Her words carried the weight of experience.

After the event, participants started exchanging contact information. A small network was formed. Garu-chan listened to her child's gentle snores in her pouch while writing down the theme for the next event. 'Let's not make family building a lonely battle.'
